Chapter 7 - Infant, Child, and Adolescen.definition: Humans are born with less developed brains.
Chapter 7 - Infant, Child, and Adolescen.important: Allows more environmental shaping and adaptability

Chapter 7 - Infant, Child, and Adolescen.definition: Time when brain is highly sensitive to environment and experience.
Removal of unused synapses during adolescence.
Major brain development occurs during adolescence.
Adolescence is another key period of brain plasticity.
Imaging technique that studies white matter quality.
Differences in gray matter and myelination.

before_30: Gray matter increases (left temporal lobe: memory/language)
Myelin in visual, auditory, limbic areas
after_30: Myelin more in frontal and parietal areas

Chapter 7 - Infant, Child, and Adolescen.definition: Brain’s ability to change and adapt.
Brain develops using common universal experiences.
Brain changes based on unique personal experiences.
Chapter 7 - Infant, Child, and Adolescen.example: Violinists show enhanced brain areas for left-hand use
